title: Leaderboard Playground
description: Mod ideas to extend this template. Each one demonstrates a different layer of the Polkadot stack and reuses the existing game/scoreboard separation.

quests.title: Swap the game
quests.summary: Replace Flappy Bird with your own game. Build a component that accepts GameComponentProps and calls onGameEnd(score) once when the game ends. The scoreboard, leaderboard UI, and player input all keep working without changes — App.tsx is the only file you need to edit.
acceptance: A new game component lives under src/games/<your-game>/
Swapping it in App.tsx is a one-line change

quests.title: Move the leaderboard on-chain
quests.summary: Implement ScoreboardAPI against a PVM smart contract on Asset Hub. Drop the new implementation into App.tsx in place of localScoreboard. The game and leaderboard UI never know the difference.
teaches: Mapping<Address, u128> for player → best score
Querying contract storage from the frontend
acceptance: Contract deployed to Paseo Asset Hub
Scores persist across browsers and accounts

quests.title: Persist game history on Bulletin
quests.summary: Each match's full history (rounds, timestamps, replay data) gets uploaded to Bulletin Chain on game end. The contract stores only (player, score, latest_cid) — heavy data lives off-chain, content-addressed.
teaches: Splitting hot index from cold replay data
acceptance: Each submitted score is paired with a Bulletin CID
Clicking a leaderboard entry fetches and replays the match

quests.title: Cross-game scoring
quests.summary: Add a game_id field to ScoreEntry and ScoreboardAPI so multiple games can share one leaderboard contract. Players accumulate points across all games; per-game leaderboards are still available via filter.
teaches: API evolution without breaking existing games
acceptance: Two distinct games write to the same scoreboard
A 'global' tab sums points across games per player
